feed.title: YONMILK
feed.subtitle: Personal blog sharing thoughts, experiences, and discoveries

entry.title: Welcome to my blog
entry.content:
Welcome to My Blog

Hello and welcome! I’m excited to share this space with you where I’ll be documenting my journey, thoughts, and discoveries.

What You Can Expect

This blog will be a mix of:

  • Technology insights - Sharing what I learn about web development, programming, and tech trends
  • Design thoughts - Exploring minimal design principles and user experience
  • Life reflections - Personal observations and daily musings
  • Learning notes - Documentation of my coding adventures and new skills

Why I Started This Blog

I believe in the power of sharing knowledge and experiences. Writing helps me organize my thoughts and hopefully provides value to others on similar journeys.

Let’s Connect

Feel free to reach out if you have questions or want to discuss any of the topics I cover. I’m always eager to learn from others and engage in meaningful conversations.

Thank you for visiting, and I hope you find something valuable here!
entry.summary: This is where I share my thoughts, experiences, and discoveries. Join me on this journey of learning and growth.

entry.title: My journey into web development
entry.content:
My Journey Into Web Development

When I first started learning web development, I had no idea what I was getting into. What seemed like a simple way to create websites turned into a fascinating journey of continuous learning.

The Beginning

It all started with curiosity about how websites work. I remember viewing the source of my favorite sites and being amazed by the code that created what I saw on screen.

My first steps:

  • Learning HTML basics
  • Understanding CSS styling
  • Getting frustrated with JavaScript (we’ve all been there!)

Key Discoveries

1. The Power of Modern Frameworks

Moving from vanilla HTML/CSS/JS to modern frameworks was a game-changer. The component-based thinking changed how I approach building interfaces.

2. The Importance of Developer Experience

Good tooling makes all the difference. From code editors with intelligent autocomplete to build tools that handle optimization, the developer experience has come so far.

3. The Community

The web development community is incredibly welcoming and helpful. Open source projects, tutorials, and forums have been invaluable resources.

Current Focus

These days I’m diving deeper into:

  • Static site generators like Jekyll
  • Modern CSS techniques
  • Performance optimization
  • Accessibility best practices

What’s Next

The journey continues! Next on my learning list:

  • Advanced JavaScript patterns
  • Web performance metrics
  • Progressive Web Apps

The best part about web development? There’s always something new to learn!
entry.summary: How I started learning web development and what I discovered along the way.

entry.title: What I learned about minimal design

link.title: What I learned about minimal design

entry.content:
What I Learned About Minimal Design

Minimal design isn’t just about making things look clean – it’s a philosophy that prioritizes function over form while still maintaining visual appeal.

Core Principles

Less is More

Every element should serve a purpose. If it doesn’t add value, it should be removed.

White Space is Your Friend

White space (or negative space) gives content room to breathe and helps guide the user’s attention to what matters most.

Typography Matters

With fewer visual elements, typography becomes crucial. The right font choice and hierarchy can carry the entire design.

Practical Applications

In Web Design

  • Clean navigation structures
  • Generous margins and padding
  • Limited color palettes
  • Focus on readability

In User Interfaces

  • Clear call-to-action buttons
  • Logical information hierarchy
  • Consistent spacing systems
  • Reduced cognitive load

Common Mistakes

  1. Confusing minimal with boring - Minimal can still be engaging and interesting
  2. Removing too much - Essential information shouldn’t be sacrificed for aesthetics
  3. Ignoring accessibility - Minimal doesn’t mean hard to use
  4. Inconsistent spacing - Without decorative elements, spacing becomes more noticeable

Inspiration Sources

Some of my favorite examples of minimal design:

  • Apple’s product pages
  • Medium’s reading experience
  • Stripe’s documentation
  • Ghost’s publishing platform

The Challenge

The real challenge of minimal design is knowing what to keep and what to remove. It requires deep understanding of user needs and business goals.

As Antoine de Saint-Exupéry said: “Perfection is achieved not when there is nothing more to add, but when there is nothing left to take away.”
entry.summary: Exploring the principles of minimal design and how less can truly be more.

entry.title: Building my first static blog
entry.content:
Building My First Static Blog

After years of using various blogging platforms, I decided it was time to build something myself. Here’s why I chose to go the static site route and what I learned in the process.

Why Static?

Performance

Static sites are incredibly fast. No database queries, no server processing – just pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript served directly to the browser.

Security

With no database or server-side processing, the attack surface is minimal. Static sites are inherently more secure.

Simplicity

No need to worry about server maintenance, database backups, or plugin updates. Just focus on content.

Cost

Hosting is cheap (or free) with services like GitHub Pages, Netlify, or Vercel.

Technology Choices

Jekyll

I chose Jekyll for its:

  • Native GitHub Pages support
  • Mature ecosystem
  • Liquid templating
  • Built-in blog structure

Design Approach

I wanted something clean and inspired by Apple’s design language:

  • Minimal color palette
  • Clean typography
  • Card-based layouts
  • Responsive design

The Build Process

1. Structure Planning

First, I mapped out the site structure:

  • Home page with post grid
  • Individual post pages
  • Navigation and footer
  • Responsive layouts

2. Styling

I focused on:

  • Typography hierarchy
  • Consistent spacing
  • Hover effects and transitions
  • Mobile-first responsive design

3. Content Strategy

Planning for different post types:

  • Technical tutorials
  • Design insights
  • Personal reflections
  • Project showcases

Challenges Faced

Learning Liquid Templating

Coming from modern JavaScript frameworks, Liquid felt different but proved quite powerful for static site generation.

CSS Organization

Without a CSS framework, I had to think carefully about organizing styles for maintainability.

Image Optimization

Static sites require more manual work for image optimization, but the performance benefits are worth it.

Deployment

GitHub Pages makes deployment incredibly simple:

  1. Push to main branch
  2. GitHub builds and deploys automatically
  3. Site is live at username.github.io

What’s Next

Plans for future improvements:

  • Add search functionality
  • Implement dark mode
  • Add comment system
  • Optimize for Core Web Vitals

Building a static blog was a great learning experience and reminded me why I love web development – the ability to create something from nothing with just code!
entry.summary: The process of creating a static blog and why I chose this approach.

entry.title: Daily reflections and observations
entry.content:
Daily Reflections and Observations

Sometimes the most profound insights come from the quietest moments. Here are some recent observations from daily life.

Morning Routines

There’s something powerful about starting the day intentionally. I’ve been experimenting with different morning routines and noticed how much they influence the rest of the day.

What works for me:

  • No phone for the first hour
  • A few minutes of quiet reflection
  • Planning three important tasks
  • Starting with the most challenging work

The Rhythm of Creativity

I’ve been paying attention to my creative energy throughout the day. Interesting patterns emerge:

Morning (6-10 AM): Best for deep, focused work Midday (10 AM-2 PM): Good for collaborative tasks Afternoon (2-5 PM): Perfect for administrative work
Evening (5-8 PM): Ideal for learning and reading

Small Wins Matter

Today I celebrated fixing a CSS bug that had been bothering me for days. It reminded me that progress isn’t always about big achievements – sometimes it’s about solving the small problems that were blocking the path forward.

The Value of Constraints

Working within limitations often produces better results than having unlimited options. Whether it’s a design project with specific requirements or a coding challenge with certain restrictions, constraints force creativity.

Digital Minimalism

I’ve been reducing digital noise:

  • Unsubscribed from newsletters I never read
  • Cleaned up my desktop
  • Organized bookmarks into meaningful categories
  • Reduced notification interruptions

The result? More mental clarity and focus.

Questions I’m Pondering

  • How do we balance productivity with presence?
  • What makes some learning stick while other information fades?
  • Why do we often overcomplicate simple solutions?
  • How can technology serve us better instead of distracting us?

Tonight’s Gratitude

  • A productive day of coding
  • Learning something new about Jekyll
  • Good coffee and quiet morning hours
  • The satisfaction of clean, working code

These small moments and reflections add up to something bigger. Life is in the details.
entry.summary: Small moments and thoughts that make up the bigger picture of daily life.

entry.title: Notes from my coding adventures
entry.content:
Notes from My Coding Adventures

Every coding session teaches something new. Here are some notes and lessons from recent development work.

Today’s Bug Hunt

The Case of the Mysterious CSS Overflow

Problem: Content was mysteriously cutting off on mobile devices.

Investigation:

  • Checked responsive breakpoints ✓
  • Verified container widths ✓
  • Examined z-index issues ✓
  • Found it! A white-space: nowrap causing horizontal overflow

Lesson: Sometimes the simplest properties cause the most confusing bugs.

JavaScript Scope Gotcha

Problem: Variables weren’t updating as expected in a loop.

Discovery: Classic closure issue in a setTimeout callback.

// Problem
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
  setTimeout(() => console.log(i), 1000); // Always logs items.length
}

// Solution  
for (let i = 0; i < items.length; i++) {
  setTimeout((index) => console.log(index), 1000, i);
}

Lesson: let vs var matters, but closures can still trip you up.

New Things Learned

Git Aliases Save Time

Set up some useful Git shortcuts:

git config --global alias.co checkout
git config --global alias.br branch  
git config --global alias.ci commit
git config --global alias.st status

Small efficiency gains add up over time.

CSS Grid is Amazing

Finally dove deeper into CSS Grid. The ability to create complex layouts with clean, semantic HTML is incredible.

.grid-container {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 1fr;
  grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;
  gap: 20px;
}

Much cleaner than the float and flexbox workarounds I used to rely on.

Problem-Solving Patterns

The Rubber Duck Method Works

Explaining problems out loud (even to a rubber duck) really does help. Speaking forces you to organize thoughts clearly.

Break It Down

When stuck on a complex problem:

  1. Identify the smallest possible step
  2. Get that working first
  3. Build up incrementally
  4. Test frequently

Read the Error Messages

Sounds obvious, but I catch myself jumping to solutions before carefully reading what the error actually says.

Tools and Techniques

Browser DevTools Gems

  • console.table() for displaying arrays/objects
  • $0 to reference the currently selected element
  • Network tab throttling to test slow connections
  • Lighthouse for performance audits

Keyboard Shortcuts That Changed My Life

  • Cmd/Ctrl + D - Select next occurrence (VS Code)
  • Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P - Command palette
  • Alt + Click - Multiple cursors
  • Cmd/Ctrl + / - Toggle line comment

Reflection

The more I code, the more I realize how much there is to learn. But that’s what makes it exciting – there’s always another problem to solve, another technique to master, another “aha!” moment waiting.

The key is embracing the learning process and celebrating small victories along the way.

Tomorrow’s Goals

  • Refactor that messy component I wrote last week
  • Learn about CSS custom properties
  • Practice more Git branching strategies
  • Write better commit messages

The adventure continues!
entry.summary: Lessons learned, bugs squashed, and aha moments from recent coding sessions.
